symptom: Intermittent cooling with audible clicking from the outdoor unit. Compressor attempts to start then trips out after a few seconds. Short cycling pattern.
diagnosis: Failed or weak dual-run capacitor. Compressor cannot achieve starting torque, trips internal overload.
procedure: Kill power at the disconnect and the breaker. Verify with non-contact tester.
Remove the outdoor unit access panel (4 sheet-metal screws).
Discharge the existing capacitor across HERM and C terminals with an insulated 20k ohm resistor.
Note wire colors: Yellow to HERM (compressor), Brown to FAN, Black to C (common).
Measure capacitance on both sides with a multimeter in capacitance mode. Spec: 45 uF (HERM) / 5 uF (FAN), ±6%.
If either side reads >6% off spec, replace with P291-4554RS (45/5 uF 440V).
Reconnect wires to matching terminals. Secure with the original retaining bracket.
Restore power. Verify compressor starts without clicking; listen for 3 full cooling cycles.
Confirm supply air temp drop of 15-20°F across the air handler.
safety_notes: Capacitors store lethal voltage even when power is off — always discharge before touching terminals.
Verify disconnect is pulled AND breaker is off. Tag if needed.
Bulging or leaking capacitors indicate failure; handle with gloves.
